articles.title: Amy Winehouse's Dad Loses Court Case Over Auction
articles.summary: Amy Winehouse was a famous singer who died in 2011. Her dad, Mitch Winehouse, went to court because he thought two of Amy's friends, Naomi Parry and Catriona Gourlay, sold Amy's things at an auction without telling him. He wanted to stop them and get answers. The judge, Sarah Clarke, said Mitch lost the case. She said he could have found out about the items earlier if he tried harder. The judge also said Mitch cares a lot about protecting Amy's memory and making sure the family gets money from her things. Amy's friends said Amy gave them the items as gifts because she was very generous and didn't want to wear the same clothes twice. The court agreed with the friends, so Mitch did not win.

articles.title: Museums Compete for a Big Prize
articles.summary: Five museums in the UK are finalists for the Art Fund Museum of the Year award, which gives a prize of £120,000. The museums are Norwich Castle Museum and Art Gallery, Fitzwilliam Museum in Cambridge, The Box in Plymouth, The National Gallery in London, and V&A East Storehouse in London. They were chosen because they show great creativity and new ideas. The director of Art Fund, Jenny Waldman, hopes people will visit these museums and their local ones. Norwich Castle reopened after five years of work and now shows life from the 12th century. The Box tells the history of Plymouth. The winner will be announced on June 25 at a ceremony in London, and the other four finalists will each get £20,000. The prize money comes from Art Fund members who buy a National Art Pass and supporters. Last year, Beamish museum won.

articles.title: Arthur Fils Wins Tennis Trophy in Barcelona
articles.summary: Arthur Fils, a 21-year-old tennis player from France, won a big tournament in Barcelona. He beat Andrey Rublev from Russia in the final match. Arthur had been hurt for eight months and couldn't play, but he came back strong. He thanked his team for helping him recover. In another part of the tournament, Julian Cash and Lloyd Glasspool won the doubles title. They are from Britain and had won at Wimbledon before. This win in Barcelona was their first trophy this year. Arthur is happy to be playing again and winning.

articles.title: Elena Rybakina Wins Tennis Tournament in Stuttgart
articles.summary: Elena Rybakina, a tennis player from Kazakhstan, won a big tennis match in Stuttgart, Germany. She played against Karolina Muchova from the Czech Republic and won in two sets with scores of 7-5 and 6-1. The match took about one hour and 18 minutes. This is her second win in 2026, after she also won the Australian Open earlier in the year. Rybakina has won this Stuttgart tournament before in 2024. As a prize for winning, she gets a Porsche car, but last time she couldn't drive it because she didn't have a license. She thanked her team for helping her improve. Muchova did well too, winning a tournament in Qatar recently, and this will help her ranking.

articles.title: Fun Soccer Game Raises Money for Charity
articles.summary: A big soccer game called the Sidemen charity match happened at Wembley Stadium. It was sold out with many people watching. The game ended in a tie, and the YouTube Allstars won in a penalty shootout. This event raised £6.2 million for charities like Bright Side and M7 Education. There were fun moments, like a player making a yellow card catch fire and a singer performing at halftime. Over two million people watched it online. The Sidemen say it's popular because it's not too serious and everyone has a good time while helping others.

articles.title: Emma Raducanu Skips Another Tennis Tournament
articles.summary: Emma Raducanu is a famous tennis player from Britain who won the US Open when she was a teenager. She has not played a match since early March because she has been sick with a viral illness. Now, she has decided not to play in the Madrid Open tennis tournament, which means she will miss more games. Raducanu needs time to get better and practice on clay courts, which are a type of tennis surface that can be hard on her back. She has had back problems before on clay. Her next chance to play on clay might be in Rome in May. If she doesn't play soon, she might have a long break from tennis, which could make it tough for her when she tries to play on grass courts later in the year. Raducanu is working on her fitness and hopes to return strong.
